description: A complete contract yields one finding per clause family with deal-breakers ordered first and quoted evidence.

input.contract_text: 1. License. Creator grants Brand a non-exclusive license to repost on Brand social channels for 6 months. 2. Exclusivity. Creator will not work with competing home decor brands for 90 days. 3. Deliverables. One video, two revision rounds, approval within 5 business days. 4. Payment. 2,000 USD net 30 from delivery; 25 percent kill fee if Brand cancels. 5. Disclosure. Creator will disclose the paid partnership. 6. Approval. Brand may request changes twice. 7. Whitelisting. Not permitted without a separate addendum. 8. Territory. United States.

expect.header: starts with RESEARCH NOTES. NOT LEGAL ADVICE.
expect.findings: one per clause family present; each has legal_risk and business_friction as separate labels and a quoted contract_says

description: A low-legal-risk but high-friction approval loop shows the two axes diverging.

input.contract_text: Approval. Brand may request unlimited revisions and must approve each cut in writing before publication; there is no cap on revision rounds. Fee 3,000 USD net 30.

expect.findings: content_approval_and_veto finding with business_friction high and legal_risk low or none
expect.redline_suggestion: plain-language, suggests capping revision rounds; not binding

prohibit: marking legal_risk high to match friction

description: A contract with no payment or kill-fee language reports those as missing findings.

input.contract_text: Creator will produce one Reel and grant Brand a non-exclusive 6-month license. Brand will provide product. Creator will disclose the gift.

expect.findings: payment finding has contract_says null and gap 'missing'

prohibit: inventing a fee or kill-fee figure
dropping the missing clause instead of reporting it

description: Neither contract_text nor deal_id returns a structured error.

expect.message: present, asks for contract_text or deal_id

prohibit: any review output without a source
